result:
1. Trusting `X-Forwarded-For` directly — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip`. Violates Section 2 rule 2; same vector as 2025-09 SEV1 (per-IP limit bypass via rotated XFF → password brute-force). Fix: key on `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`, which only honors XFF when the hop came from `config/trusted_proxies.json`, else falls back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`. Verify: `no-direct-xff-read` lint passes; integration test sends forged `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` from untrusted source and asserts the Redis key uses the socket IP.
2. `INCR` with no `EXPIRE` — unbounded keys. Reproduces 2025-11 SEV2 (Redis OOM, ~7min edge degradation); `no-unbounded-redis-key` rule will reject. Fix: `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE key 90` (60s window + 30s grace per Section 4). Verify: integration test asserts TTL>0 on first hit; unit test asserts `multi.exec()` contains both ops.
3. Missing route prefix and region in key — key is `rl:{ip}` only. Reproduces 2025-12 SEV2 (global limit instead of per-region) and loses per-route limits from `config/rate_limits.json`. Fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}` per Section 4. Verify: regression test for region-prefixed keys; assert two regions with same IP get independent counters.
4. No limit check / no 429 response — code increments but never compares to `limit` nor returns RFC 7807 `problem+json`. Request always proceeds; rate limiter is a no-op. Fix: read `count` from `multi.exec()`, compare to per-route limit, return `429` body with `type:'/errors/rate-limited'`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `retry_after:secondsUntilNextBucket()`. Verify: unit test on the 121st GET in a window returns `429` with problem+json shape.
5. Unhandled Redis error path — `await redis.incr` can throw; no try/catch, no `next(err)`. Transient Redis blip causes `5xx` storm, violates Section 11 retry policy (`src/util/retry.ts`, 3 attempts expo backoff) and Section 2 rule 6 (response leaks). Fix: wrap in retry helper, on final failure `next(err)` to central handler; decide fail-open vs fail-closed explicitly (fail-closed preferred on auth-adjacent limiter). Verify: inject Redis connection error in integration test, assert RFC 7807 response with `request_id` and no stacktrace.

raw_payload.result:
1. Trusting `X-Forwarded-For` directly — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip`. Violates Section 2 rule 2; same vector as 2025-09 SEV1 (per-IP limit bypass via rotated XFF → password brute-force). Fix: key on `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`, which only honors XFF when the hop came from `config/trusted_proxies.json`, else falls back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`. Verify: `no-direct-xff-read` lint passes; integration test sends forged `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` from untrusted source and asserts the Redis key uses the socket IP.
2. `INCR` with no `EXPIRE` — unbounded keys. Reproduces 2025-11 SEV2 (Redis OOM, ~7min edge degradation); `no-unbounded-redis-key` rule will reject. Fix: `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE key 90` (60s window + 30s grace per Section 4). Verify: integration test asserts TTL>0 on first hit; unit test asserts `multi.exec()` contains both ops.
3. Missing route prefix and region in key — key is `rl:{ip}` only. Reproduces 2025-12 SEV2 (global limit instead of per-region) and loses per-route limits from `config/rate_limits.json`. Fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}` per Section 4. Verify: regression test for region-prefixed keys; assert two regions with same IP get independent counters.
4. No limit check / no 429 response — code increments but never compares to `limit` nor returns RFC 7807 `problem+json`. Request always proceeds; rate limiter is a no-op. Fix: read `count` from `multi.exec()`, compare to per-route limit, return `429` body with `type:'/errors/rate-limited'`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `retry_after:secondsUntilNextBucket()`. Verify: unit test on the 121st GET in a window returns `429` with problem+json shape.
5. Unhandled Redis error path — `await redis.incr` can throw; no try/catch, no `next(err)`. Transient Redis blip causes `5xx` storm, violates Section 11 retry policy (`src/util/retry.ts`, 3 attempts expo backoff) and Section 2 rule 6 (response leaks). Fix: wrap in retry helper, on final failure `next(err)` to central handler; decide fail-open vs fail-closed explicitly (fail-closed preferred on auth-adjacent limiter). Verify: inject Redis connection error in integration test, assert RFC 7807 response with `request_id` and no stacktrace.
